## Overview

Made Tech Group plc (LSE: MTEC) is a UK-listed (Aim) digital, data, and technology services provider focused on the public sector. The company has a strong presence in central government, healthcare, and local government, with emphasis on digital transformation, legacy modernisation, and data-driven decision-making. As a comparator for ArcKit Consulting, Made Tech is the closest structural analogue (UK-only, public-sector-only, transparent rate cards, LSE-listed) but does **not** offer an explicit SME-tier cross-subsidy commitment — the differentiator that ArcKit Consulting brings.

## Products & Services

- **Digital transformation** — service design, agile delivery, user research, multidisciplinary teams.
- **Legacy modernisation** — migration of legacy services to cloud-native architectures.
- **Data and AI** — data engineering, analytics, ML / AI advisory.
- **Quality Assurance Testing** — QAT 2 framework (CCS) participation; Made Tech can run QA testing for all UK central government departments and other public sector organisations.
- **Cyber and security advisory** — covered partly under their Strategic IT and Security Delivery Partner role with GDS.

## Pricing Model

- Day-rate / fixed-price hybrid.
- Transparent rate cards published on G-Cloud listings.
- Engagement size typically £100k–£20m; major contracts (e.g., £19m GDS Strategic IT and Security Delivery Partner contract awarded 2026) are in the £10m+ band.

## UK Government Presence

- G-Cloud listed: Yes (Made Tech is listed across multiple G-Cloud iterations including Lots 2 and 3).
- DOS listed: Yes (Made Tech is a DOS specialist and outcomes supplier).
- MCF listed: Listed under multiple frameworks (MCF participation; QAT 2 etc).
- Public sector focus: 90%+ revenue from UK public sector.
- UK data centres: Yes — UK-resident operations.

## Strengths

- **LSE-listed transparency** — Companies House and LSE filings provide verifiable financial standing.
- **Pure public-sector focus** — strong domain knowledge across central government, NHS, local authorities.
- **Recent major contract win** — £19m three-year contract with GDS as Strategic IT and Security Delivery Partner (2026), demonstrating market position and buyer confidence.
- **Transparent rate cards** on G-Cloud listings.
- **Quality posture** — investment in QA testing capability (QAT 2 framework participation).
- **Scale to support major framework participation** — financial standing meets stricter framework eligibility criteria that smaller SMEs cannot.

## Weaknesses

- **Plc dividend / total shareholder return pressure** — structurally limits the ability to fund a deep SME-tier cross-subsidy of the kind ArcKit Consulting commits to.
- **No explicit SME-tier commitment visible in public communications** — Made Tech serves SME suppliers indirectly but does not offer a structured SME-affordable engagement tier.
- **Scale advantage** is a barrier to ArcKit Consulting on prime contracts, but ArcKit Consulting's positioning is on different ground (SME suppliers + departmental advisory, not £10m+ transformation programmes).

## Comparison to ArcKit Consulting Positioning

| Dimension | Made Tech | ArcKit Consulting (Target) |
|-----------|-----------|---------------------------|
| Legal vehicle | Ltd / LSE plc | Ltd → B-Corp (year 2) |
| Public sector focus | 90%+ | 100% (with SME suppliers as the SME tier) |
| Transparent rate card | Yes | Yes (mirroring) |
| SME-tier commitment | No | Yes (Principle 1, externally verifiable) |
| Toolkit-anchored | No (custom delivery) | Yes (ArcKit toolkit as delivery medium) |
| Engagement size sweet spot | £100k–£20m | £20k–£500k (SME tier) + £100k–£2m (standard) |
| Scale | ~300+ employees | 10–30 employees (target year 3) |
